reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails to incorporate the requested non-linguistic vocalization (laughter). While the voice has a generally pleasant and slightly smiling intonation, it strictly adheres to reading the text without generating the actual sound of laughter or modifying the speech to sound like it is being spoken through laughter. reasoning_model_2: Model 2 successfully integrates the requested non-linguistic vocalization. It begins with a clear, natural-sounding burst of laughter (0.0s - 1.5s) before delivering the line. model_comparison: The user explicitly requested the sentence to be read 'with laughter', targeting Non-Linguistic Vocalizations. Model 1 failed this specific instruction completely; it delivered a clean reading with a happy tone but zero audible laughter. Model 2, in contrast, generated a distinct and natural laugh before the speech and maintained the amused affect throughout the sentence. Model 2 demonstrates superior control over paralinguistic features.
